items.title: WSJ узнала о смягчении Ираном требований в переговорах с США
items.summary: Ранее Иран настаивал на снятии американской морской блокады для начала переговоров. Теперь, по данным WSJ, Тегеран готов обсудить открытие Ормузского пролива одновременно с ее снятием

items.title: US Dollar Index rebounds from two-week lows as as tariff tensions resurface
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which tracks the Greenback’s value against a basket of six major currencies, turns higher on Friday, recovering from earlier weakness, though it remains on track to close the week in negative territory.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average futures hold near 50K as Iran offer offsets tariff noise
items.summary: Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) futures traded above 49,800 in overnight and pre-market action, building on a near 1,500-point rally from Thursday morning's lows around 48,500 and pressing toward the 50,000 mark for the first time.

items.title: Forecasting the upcoming week: Nonfarm Payrolls, US-Iran peace talks on focus as DXY hits two-week lows
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell to a two-week low near the 98.00 price zone on Friday, extending the losses seen the previous day and ending a week in which the world's most important central banks confirmed a hawkish shift due to rising inflation pressures.

items.title: US President Trump: I will be increasing tariffs on vehicles imported from the European Union
items.summary: Donald Trump, United States President, announced on Friday through Truth Social a new round of tariffs on the European Union (EU) for cars and trucks.

items.title: WTI Crude falls as Iranian proposal raises hopes for renewed US-Iran negotiations
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude Oil eases on Friday, trimming part of the strong gains registered earlier this week as renewed diplomatic efforts to end the US-Iran war lift market sentiment.

items.title: US Dollar Index posts modest gains above 98.00 on hawkish Fed pause despite weaker US GDP
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 98.15 during the Asian trading hours on Friday. The DXY posts modest gains on a hawkish hold from the US Federal Reserve (Fed).

items.title: Fitch заявило о новых рисках стабильности экономики США из-за Ирана
items.summary: Расходы на войну с Ираном представляют для США дополнительные финансовые риски. Вашингтон уже потратил на военную операцию $25 млрд

items.title: Canadian Dollar sits near its highest level since March 11 vs USD amid elevated Oil prices
items.summary: The USD/CAD pair enters a bearish consolidation phase after touching a fresh low since March 11 during the Asian session on Friday, and currently trades around the 1.3575 region. Nevertheless, spot prices remain on track to register losses for the fourth straight week.

items.title: WTI rises to near $102.50 amid rising supply concerns from Middle East
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price inches higher after registering 2.7% losses in the previous day, trading around $102.40 per barrel during the Asian hours on Friday.

items.title: Australian Dollar weakens as Middle East tensions boost US Dollar
items.summary: The AUD/USD pair drifts lower to near 0.7200 during the early Asian trading hours on Friday. Ongoing tensions in the Middle East continue to boost a safe-haven currency such as the US Dollar (USD) against the Aussie.

items.title: Japanese Yen weakens as softer Tokyo CPI and Iran tensions counter intervention warnings
items.summary: The USD/JPY pair is seen building on the previous day's late rebound from the vicinity of mid-155.00s, or over a two-month low, and gaining some positive traction during the Asian session on Friday.

items.title: NZD/USD holds losses near 0.5900 as NZ Consumer Confidence Falls in April
items.summary: NZD/USD pulls back after registering over 1.25% gains in the previous day, trading around 0.5900 during the Asian hours on Friday. The pair depreciates as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) remains subdued following the release of weaker mid-tier economic data from the country.

items.title: PBOC sets USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8628 vs. 6.8608 previous
items.summary: The People’s Bank of China (PBOC) sets the USD/CNY central rate for the trading session ahead on Friday at 6.8628 compared to the previous day's fix of 6.8608.

items.title: Japan’s Mimura: In close contact with US on foreign exchange
items.summary: Atsushi Mimura, Japan’s Vice Finance Minister for International Affairs and top foreign exchange official, said on Friday that he had no comment on foreign exchange intervention and crude oil futures. Mimura added that the official is in close contact with the United States (US) on currency.

items.title: US Dollar Index holds steady after Fed hold, traders await US GDP and PCE data
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 98.95 during the Asian trading hours on Thursday. The DXY steadies following a hawkish hold from the US Federal Reserve (Fed).

items.title: Canadian Dollar receives support despite lower oil prices
items.summary: USD/CAD edges lower after remaining flat in the previous day, trading around 1.3680 during the Asian hours on Thursday.

items.title: Japanese Yen gathers strength amid intervention fears
items.summary: The USD/JPY pair loses traction to around 160.25 during the Asian trading hours on Thursday. The Japanese Yen (JPY) edges higher against the US Dollar (USD) amid intervention fears from Japanese authorities.

items.title: NZD/USD rises to near 0.5850 ahead of Chinese PMI data
items.summary: NZD/USD gains ground after two days of losses, trading around 0.5830 during the Asian hours on Thursday. The pair advances as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) remains stronger following the latest domestic economic data releases.

items.title: Демократы начали готовить судебный иск против Трампа из-за войны с Ираном
items.summary: 1 мая истекает 60-дневном лимит на ведение боевых действий без санкции Конгресса. После этой даты Трамп обязан заручиться подержкой конгрессменов, либо начать сворачивать операцию против Тегерана

items.title: Australian Dollar edges higher ahead of Chinese PMI releases
items.summary: The AUD/USD pair gathers strength to near 0.7130 during the early Asian session on Thursday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) edges higher against the US Dollar (USD) on hotter domestic inflation data.

items.title: WSJ рассказала, как война и экономический спад ударили по простым иранцам
items.summary: Иранцы уже ощущают на себе последствия спада в экономике страны из-за войны, пишет The Wall Street Journal.

items.title: US Dollar Index steadies near 98.50, all eyes on Fed rate decision
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), an index of the value of the US Dollar (USD) measured against a basket of six world currencies, currently trades near 98.65 during the Asian trading hours on Wednesday.

items.title: Canadian Dollar consolidates vs USD as traders keenly await BoC/FOMC rate decisions
items.summary: The USD/CAD pair seesaws between tepid gains/minor losses, below the 1.3700 mark, during the Asian session on Wednesday amid a combination of diverging forces. The lack of progress in US-Iran peace talks continues to benefit the US Dollar's (USD) reserve currency status and supports spot prices.

items.title: RBNZ’s Breman: Q1 core inflation have remained within the 1 - 3% target band
items.summary: Reserve Bank of New Zealand (RBNZ) Governor Anna Breman said on Wednesday that “the first quarter measures of core inflation have remained stable within the target band of 1–3%.”

items.title: AUD/JPY holds losses near 114.50 after Australian CPI inflation data
items.summary: The AUD/JPY cross declines to around 114.50 during the early Asian trading hours on Wednesday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) softens against the Japanese Yen (JPY) following the release of the Australian inflation report.

items.title: Australian Dollar drifts lower after CPI report; focus shifts to Fed decision
items.summary: The AUD/USD pair continues with its struggle to conquer the 0.7200 mark and drifts lower following the release of Australian consumer inflation figures during the Asian session on Wednesday.

items.title: Breaking: Australia’s CPI inflation climbs to 4.6% YoY in March vs. 4.7% expected
items.summary: Australia’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) climbed by 4.6% year-over-year (YoY) in March, compared to a 3.7% increase reported in the previous reading, the latest data published by the Australian Bureau of Statistics (ABS) showed on Wednesday.

items.title: WTI rises to near $98.00 as global supply risks escalate
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price gains ground for the third successive day, trading around $96.90 per barrel during the Asian hours on Wednesday.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average futures wobble as Iran talks stall, Crude Oil jumps
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) sees marginal gains on Tuesday's session, with futures holding near 49,200 and the underlying cash index adding roughly 0.1%, propped up by a 5% jump in Coca-Cola (KO) after a stronger-than-expected earnings print.

items.title: BOJ: Gradual tightening with higher inflation risks – UOB
items.summary: United Overseas Bank’s Alvin Liew reports the Bank of Japan (BoJ) left its policy rate at 0.75% but signalled that the next move will be a hike as underlying inflation nears target and real rates stay very low.

items.title: Fed: Data-dependent path toward neutral – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir argue that new inflation tools championed by incoming Fed Chair Kevin Warsh, including trimmed mean measures and a potential big-data price project, will not materially alter the policy outlook.

items.title: WTI rises to near $95.50 as Strait of Hormuz remains shut
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price extends its gains for the second successive day, trading around $95.20 per barrel during the Asian hours on Tuesday. Crude oil prices rise as the critical Strait of Hormuz remains largely shut, tightening Middle East energy supplies.

items.title: В России запретили продажу партии минеральной воды из Армении
items.summary: Государственная система маркировки «Честный знак» приостановила реализацию партии минеральной воды «Джермук» (Армения) в офлайн‑ и онлайн-магазинах.

items.title: Евродепутат объяснил отказ ЕС включать один пункт в меры против России
items.summary: Евросоюз отказался от полного запрета на морские перевозки российской нефти в 20-м пакете санкций против России из-за экономической ситуации и риска конфликта с партнерами, заявил депутат Европарламента от Люксембурга Фернан Картайзер, передает «РИА Новости».

items.title: Canadian Dollar dips vs USD; Oil strength to limit losses and cap USD/CAD
items.summary: The USD/CAD pair reverses a modest Asian session dip on Tuesday and looks to build on the previous day's modest rebound from sub-1.3600 levels, or the lowest since March 12.

items.title: PBOC sets USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8589 vs. 6.8579 previous
items.summary: The People’s Bank of China (PBOC) sets the USD/CNY central rate for the trading session ahead on Tuesday at 6.8589 compared to the previous day's fix of 6.8579 and 6.8282 Reuters estimate.

items.title: Japanese Yen extends the range play against USD; looks to BoJ for fresh impetus
items.summary: The USD/JPY pair is seen consolidating in a narrow band around mid-159.00s during the Asian session on Tuesday as traders opt to wait for the crucial Bank of Japan (BoJ) before placing fresh directional bets.

items.title: CNY: Supported by exports and geopolitics – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s FX team highlights that CNY is the only Asian currency stronger against the Dollar since late February, helped by robust exports and policy support.

items.title: NZD/USD climbs above 0.59 as hot CPI fuels RBNZ hike bets ahead of Fed
items.summary: NZD/USD posted limited gains of around 0.4% on Monday, settling close to 0.5905 but stalling just below the 0.5925 area that capped last week's recovery.

items.title: Умер посол в отставке Станислав Ахмедов
items.summary: Чрезвычайный и полномочный посол в отставке Станислав Ахмедов умер на 75-м году жизни, сообщает пресс-служба Министерства иностранных дел России.

items.title: US: Growth normalizes as Iran shock fades – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategists Oscar Munoz and Eli Nir project output growth gradually slowing to potential by late 2026 as Iran-related stagflationary risks keep the Federal Reserve (Fed) cautious.

items.title: FX Today: US Dollar falls as markets brace for central bank week and Iran headlines
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) is trading cautiously near the 98.50 level as markets prepare for a busy week of central bank meetings.

items.title: PHP: BSP starts new hiking cycle – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank highlights that BSP raised its policy rate by 25bp to 4.50%, signalling the start of a new tightening cycle to anchor inflation expectations.

items.title: PBoC: Steepening bias as industrial activity holds – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economist Samuel Tse analyses recent steepening in Chinese Yuan (CNY) rates, linking it to a ceasefire between the United States (US) and Iran and stronger-than-expected Q1 growth in China.

items.title: PHP: BSP tightening supports Peso but risks linger – OCBC
items.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong note the central bank of Philippines Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas' (BSP) 25bp hike to 4.5% and guidance that further increases are possible as inflation forecasts are revised higher and second-round effects emerge.

items.title: NZD/USD rises as US Dollar weakens on renewed US-Iran talks hopes
items.summary: The New Zealand Dollar (NZD) gains traction against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday as the Greenback weakens amid renewed hopes for US–Iran talks after stalled negotiations.

items.title: US: Revenue resilience after IEEPA ruling – Standard Chartered
items.summary: Standard Chartered economists Dan Pan and Steve Englander assess the impact of the United States (US) Supreme Court’s IEEPA ruling on US tariff revenue. They note that tariff income has fallen but remains well above pre-Liberation Day levels.

items.title: USD: Inflation shock risk from Hormuz blockade – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Derek Halpenny argues that the ongoing United States (US) blockade in the Strait of Hormuz is building a significant inflation shock for the US and globally, with Oil and input costs surging.

items.title: WTI Oil declines as fragile Iran-US talks, weakening US sentiment temper upside
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Oil trades around $92.55 at the time of writing on Friday, down 3.28% on the day, marking a notable pullback after reaching recent highs earlier in the week.

items.title: Asia FX: War Powers deadline drives cautious consolidation – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research’s Philip Wee highlights that currency markets are consolidating as the April 28 War Powers Resolution deadline approaches, with USD/CNY flattening after an earlier relief rally.

items.title: Uncertainty extends as Iran denies Ghalibaf resigned to negotiations team
items.summary: Headlines indicating that Iranian Parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has resigned from the negotiating team, according to Israel N12 News, spurred risk aversion and sent Crude Oil prices sharply up in the American session on Thursday.

items.title: WTI climbs as Hormuz tensions escalate, Ghalibaf exit rumors cloud US-Iran talks
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) Crude Oil trades higher on Thursday, extending gains for a fourth straight day as escalating tensions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to fuel concerns about global supply disruptions, keeping a geopolitical risk premium embedded in prices.

items.title: Brazil: Gradual easing path intact – Standard Chartered
items.summary: Standard Chartered’s Dan Pan expects Banco Central do Brasil (BCB) to continue its cautious easing cycle, projecting a 25bps cut at the 29 April meeting as inflation risks remain elevated.

items.title: LatAm FX: Positioning risks after strong gains – BNY
items.summary: BNY’s Geoff Yu notes that Latin American (LatAm) currencies have been the standout Emerging Markets (EM) FX performers, with holdings remaining strong throughout the conflict and supported by attractive nominal and real rates.

items.title: WTI Oil rebounds as Strait of Hormuz disruptions temper US-Iran peace optimism
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Oil rebounds on Thursday, gaining 2.50% to trade near $90.45 at the time of writing, after three consecutive days of decline.

items.title: Fed’s Miran: I favor three, maybe four cuts this year
items.summary: Stephen Miran, a member of the Federal Reserve (Fed), spoke in a moderated discussion about the United States (US) monetary policy at the Reinventing Bretton Woods Committee Global Macro Sessions in Washington, DC, on Thursday.

items.title: India: Price pressures and rupee controls – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economist Radhika Rao assesses early post-conflict data for India, highlighting stronger wholesale inflation and modestly higher CPI. She notes that the Wholesale Price Index is more sensitive to commodity and imported costs and is likely to rise further.

items.title: Fed's Williams: War shock is about prices, but also unavailable commodities
items.summary: John Williams, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) of New York, spoke in Midtown Manhattan on Thursday and said the Fed’s current interest-rate setting is well calibrated for an economy facing additional risks from the conflict in the Middle East.

items.title: Fed: Warsh confirmation risks tracked – Danske Bank
items.summary: Danske Bank analysts discuss US political risk around Federal Reserve (Fed) leadership, noting Kevin Warsh’s Senate hearing is scheduled for 21 April as part of his confirmation process as Fed chair.

items.title: ECB Accounts: Middle East risks keep ECB cautious on rates, inflation spillovers
items.summary: The European Central Bank (ECB) kept its interest rates unchanged in March, and the meeting accounts released on Thursday show a central bank determined to preserve flexibility in an environment marked by heightened uncertainty.

items.title: Australian Dollar outperforms due to risk-on mood
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) outperforms its major currency peers, except the Canadian Dollar (CAD), during the European trading session on Thursday.

items.title: India: Trade shock weighs on Rupee – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank analysts highlight that India’s March trade deficit narrowed more than expected as imports slumped, but warn this may reverse as Oil prices rise and exports face Middle East-related disruptions.

items.title: USD: Risk rally questions deeper downside – ING
items.summary: ING’s Chris Turner argues that while global risk assets are rallying and weighing on the US Dollar (USD), conditions for a sustained Dollar decline are not yet in place.

items.title: ECB’s Villeroy: Focus on April hike is premature
items.summary: European Central Bank (ECB) policymaker and governor of the Bank of France, François Villeroy de Galhau, said during European trading hours on Thursday that it is premature to price in an interest rate hike in the policy meeting later this month.

items.title: SNB Minutes: Swiss economic outlook becomes uncertain due to Middle East war
items.summary: No immediate response by the Swiss Franc (CHF) after the SNB minutes release. However, USD/CHF is rising in the European trade and has recovered its early losses, turning flat around 0.7820 as the US Dollar (USD) bounces back.

items.title: S&P 500: Record highs after geopolitical recovery – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank strategists highlight that the S&P 500 has rebounded sharply from its March 30 eight‑month low, rallying over 10% to close above 7,000 at fresh record highs.

items.title: When are the UK data releases and how could they affect GBP/USD?
items.summary: Thursday's UK economic docket features the release of the monthly GDP print, alongside the Trade Balance and Industrial Production, all of which will be published by the Office for National Statistics (ONS) at 06:00 GMT.

items.title: US Dollar Index slips below 98.00 on Middle East de-escalation hopes
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, is continuing its losing that began on April 6 and trading around 97.90 during the Asian hours on Thursday.

items.title: Australian Dollar holds gains after mixed Australian, Chinese data
items.summary: The AUD/USD pair holds positive ground around 0.7180 during the Asian trading hours on Thursday. The Australian Dollar (AUD) strengthens against the Greenback amid mixed economic data from Australian and Chinese dockets.

items.title: Canadian Dollar strengthens as risk-on mood weighs on US Dollar
items.summary: USD/CAD loses ground for the fourth successive day, trading around 1.3730 during the Asian hours on Thursday. The pair depreciates as the US Dollar (USD) continues to lose ground on improved market sentiment, driven by expectations of a potential de-escalation in the Middle East conflict.

items.title: When is the China quarterly GDP and how could it affect AUD/USD?
items.summary: The National Bureau of Statistics of China (NBS) will publish its data at 02.00 GMT on Thursday. China quarterly GDP is estimated to grow 1.3% in the first quarter (Q1), compared to an expansion of 1.2% in Q4. On an annual basis, the Chinese economy is forecast to expand 4.8% versus 4.5% prior.

items.title: Israeli Security Cabinet to discuss possible ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon — Reuters
items.summary: Israel's Security Cabinet convened to discuss a possible Lebanon ceasefire, a senior Israeli official said, over six weeks into a war with Hezbollah that spiralled out of the US-Israeli conflict with Iran, Reuters reported on Wednesday.

items.title: NZD/USD edges higher but upside capped as USD narrative dominates flows
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair is trading near the 0.5910 price region, slightly firmer on Thursday, as modest Kiwi strength meets a still-resilient US Dollar (USD) backdrop. Despite the uptick, gains remain limited as markets continue to assess risk sentiment.

items.title: Fed’s Musalem: Easing tariff impact will help lower inflation
items.summary: Alberto Musalem, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of St. Louis, said in an exclusive note he gave on Wednesday that the Oil shock caused by the Middle East war is likely feeding core inflation, he expects it to be near 3% throughout the year.

items.title: SGD: Policy-induced appreciation to counter inflation – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economists Taimur Baig and Chua Han Teng argue that recent commodity price shocks will inevitably lift inflation in Singapore, but highlight the role of the Singapore Dollar and policy buffers.

items.title: Forex Today: US Dollar stalls as Middle East tensions linger
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) struggled to build on recent gains, trading near 98.10 on Tuesday in a tight range as mixed US data and conflicting signals from yields capped momentum.

items.title: KRW: Tech cycle and NPS hedging shift back bullish case – MUFG
items.summary: MUFG’s Senior Currency Analyst Michael Wan argues that KRW could outperform in a de-escalation scenario despite vulnerability to prolonged conflict and higher Oil prices. The bank expects the strong AI and technology cycle to continue supporting South Korea.

items.title: WTI Oil holds steady as US troop buildup, Iran talks keep prices in balance
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Oil trades around $89.10 on Wednesday at the time of writing, remaining broadly stable on the day after earlier falling to a three-week low near $85.

items.title: ECB’s Nagel: As long as Hormuz is not resolved, the danger of higher inflation rises
items.summary: Joachim Nagel, President of the Bundesbank and member of the European Central Bank (ECB) spoke in an interview with Bloomberg on Wednesday. He said that questions about the Strait of Hormuz are essential, as we're between the baseline and an adverse scenario.

items.title: Oil: Supply shock sustains inflation risks – Rabobank
items.summary: Rabobank Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes that while risk sentiment has improved and the Dollar’s safe-haven bid has faded, the physical Oil market tells a different story.

items.title: ASEAN-6: Differentiated tightening paths after energy shock – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research economists Radhika Rao and Chua Han Teng assess how ASEAN-6 and India will respond to higher energy prices and renewed inflation pressures.

items.title: Eurozone: Downside risks for industry grow after war shock – ING
items.summary: ING’s Bert Colijn notes that Eurozone industrial production rose just 0.4% in February versus January, leaving output below most of 2025 levels. He highlights that higher energy prices and the Middle East war are set to weigh further on energy‑intensive industry and investment.

items.title: USD: Dominance questioned as rivals stay weak – Societe Generale
items.summary: Societe Generale’s Kit Juckes discusses whether the US Dollar’s global dominance is under threat as global imbalances widen and IMF meetings highlight systemic risks.

items.title: Oil: Price swings drive rate expectations – ING
items.summary: ING strategist Michiel Tukker stresses that current Oil volatility is feeding directly into rate expectations for the European Central Bank (ECB), Federal Reserve (Fed) and Bank of England (BoE).

items.title: DXY: War gains unwind as allies resist blockade – DBS
items.summary: DBS Group Research’s Philip Wee notes that the US Dollar (USD) has given back most of its Iran‑war related strength, with the US Dollar Index (DXY) down this month and nearing pre‑conflict levels.

items.title: Oil: War risks, Hormuz disruption and policy cushions – Rabobank
items.summary: Rabobank’s RaboResearch Global Economics & Markets team highlights that Oil prices have eased even as the IMF warns of a potential world recession if the Hormuz Strait remains closed.

items.title: USD: Mixed signals from PPI and sentiment – Danske Bank
items.summary: Danske Research Team highlights that softer-than-expected US PPI for March did not materially alter market pricing for Federal Reserve cuts, with only a one-in-three chance of easing this year.

items.title: US Dollar Index treads water above 98.00 despite growing market optimism
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which measures the value of the US Dollar (USD) against six major currencies, is halting its seven-day losing streak and trading around 98.20 during the Asian hours on Wednesday.

items.title: NZD/USD consolidates around 0.5900; close to one-month high amid peace talks optimism
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair struggles to capitalize on its strong move up witnessed over the past two days and oscillates in a narrow band near the 0.5900 mark during the Asian session on Wednesday.

items.title: Australian Dollar gains support from US–Iran talks optimism
items.summary: AUD/USD gains ground for the third successive day, trading around 0.7120 during the Asian hours on Wednesday. The pair appreciates as the Australian Dollar (AUD) receives support from improved market sentiment due to the potential for further United States (US)-Iran talks.

items.title: PBOC sets USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8582 vs. 6.8593 previous
items.summary: On Wednesday, the People’s Bank of China (PBOC) sets the USD/CNY central rate for the trading session ahead at 6.8582 compared to the previous day's fix of 6.8593 and 6.8096 Reuters estimate.

items.title: Japanese Yen bulls seem hesitant despite Iran diplomacy hopes; USD/JPY trades below 159.00
items.summary: The USD/JPY pair is seen consolidating the previous day's losses and oscillating in a narrow band below the 159.00 mark during the Asian session on Wednesday.

items.title: Singapore: Growth resilience faces external risks – DBS
items.summary: DBS economist Chua Han Teng highlights that Singapore’s 1Q26 GDP growth was resilient, with real GDP up 4.6% year-on-year, but warns that the Iran war shock and global slowdown pose downside risks.

items.title: NZD/USD climbs over half a percent but stalls at the 0.5900 handle
items.summary: NZD/USD rose over 0.55% on Tuesday, printing a session high near 0.5920 before pulling back to settle around 0.5900.

items.title: NZD/USD rises toward 0.5900 as risk sentiment improves and USD softens on US data
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair is trading with a bullish bias near the 0.5900 level on Wednesday, as the New Zealand Dollar (NZD) gains strength amid improving global risk sentiment and a weaker US Dollar (USD).

items.title: CNY: Safe-haven role grows with stronger yuan – Societe Generale
items.summary: Societe Generale analysts highlight that CNY strength has resumed, with USD/CNY nearing 6.80 for the first time in three years as China-linked tankers transit the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Forex Today: US Dollar extends slide as softer US data and Iran optimism reshape markets
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell toward the 98.10 region, reaching multi-week lows as softer inflation data and improving global sentiment led to a broad sell-off of the Greenback. Declining Oil prices and easing yields further contributed to the downward pressure.

items.title: Singapore: Imported energy shock drives MAS stance – UOB
items.summary: UOB’s Jester Koh highlights that MAS raised its 2026 core and headline inflation forecast ranges to 1.5–2.5% as imported energy costs surge. He stresses that higher Oil and gas prices will pass through to Singapore’s CPI via electricity, transport and goods.

items.title: WTI Oil retreats for third day on US-Iran diplomacy hopes, Hormuz risks cap downside
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Oil declines on Tuesday, with the barrel trading around $89.10 at the time of writing, down 3.93% on the day.

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average climbs as soft PPI, Iran ceasefire hopes lift sentiment
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) gained around 300 points, or 0.60%, trading near 48,500. The S&P 500 rose 1.1% while the Nasdaq Composite jumped 1.8%, led by a broad rally in technology names.

items.title: Bessent says Fed should wait for Warsh before cutting rates now
items.summary: The US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent crossed the wires earlier, stating that he’s “quite confident” that core prices would continue to edge lower in the US despite the Iran war, and that he’s pressing the Federal Reserve to cut rates.

items.title: BoE’s Greene: Second-round effects
items.summary: Megan Greene, external member of the Bank of England's (BoE) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC), Reuters reported on Tuesday that the United Kingdom’s (UK) economic activity was weak before the Iran war but that at the same time, the war impact is inflationary.

items.title: White House Adviser Hassett: Outlook for Fed having room to cut rates is going to be very solid
items.summary: Kevin Hassett, Senior Adviser for the White House told Fox Business on Tuesday that they expect a rapid reduction in energy prices once the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

items.title: Fed’s Goolsbee: We will get inflation to 2%
items.summary: Austan Goolsbee, President of the Federal Reserve (Fed) Bank of Chicago, told AP at the Semafor World Economy conference on Tuesday that the longer the Middle East concerns go, the cut could be pushed out of 2026.

items.title: Dow Jones futures gain as oil eases inflation, US-Iran talks eyed
items.summary: Dow Jones futures rise 0.12% to near 48,500 during European hours on Tuesday, ahead of the regular opening in the United States (US). Meanwhile, S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures also advance 0.16% and 0.28% to near 6,930 and 25,600, respectively, at the time of writing.

items.title: Australian Dollar underperforms as RBA’s Hauser warns of stagflation
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) underperforms its major currency peers, except the US Dollar (USD), during the European trading session on Tuesday.

items.title: S&P 500: Optimism returns with deal hopes – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank analysts report that the S&P 500 has recovered above its pre‑strike level as markets price a temporary conflict and potential US–Iran talks.

items.title: NZD/USD treads water above 0.5850 following China’s Trade Balance data
items.summary: NZD/USD inches lower after registering 0.5% gains in the previous day, trading around 0.5860 during the Asian hours on Tuesday. The pair remains subdued following the release of China's Trade Balance data for March.

items.title: RBA’s Hauser: Focused on preventing rise in inflation expectations
items.summary: Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser, speaking in a fireside chat earlier this Tuesday, warned that Australia is facing a difficult macroeconomic backdrop.

items.title: NZD/USD rebounds as USD weakness dominates despite geopolitical tensions
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair is pushing higher on Monday, April 13, climbing back toward the 0.5880 region as broad US Dollar (USD) weakness becomes the dominant market driver.

items.title: Asia FX: Softer start on Oil and risk – OCBC
items.summary: OCBC strategists Sim Moh Siong and Christopher Wong expect Asian FX, especially high-beta Oil importers like KRW, THB, PHP and INR, to open weaker as renewed geopolitical uncertainty supports crude and the Dollar. However, resumed Hormuz transit tempers tail risks.

items.title: Forex Today: US Dollar falls even as failed talks and Hormuz shutdown spark risk-off wave
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell on Monday, as markets digest headlines of failed Iran-United States (US) peace talks over the weekend and reports that US President Donald Trump moved the US Navy to close the Strait of Hormuz, a development that would typically fuel a strong safe-haven bid for the Gre

items.title: Renminbi: Limited Petroyuan prospects in energy trade – Commerzbank
items.summary: Commerzbank’s Head of FX and Commodity Research Thu Lan Nguyen examines Iran’s proposal to charge Strait of Hormuz tolls in Renminbi and finds it unlikely to trigger a major Petroyuan shift.

items.title: Trump says Iran wants a deal as Hormuz blockade officially begins
items.summary: US President Donald Trump spoke at a press conference at the Oval Office and praised Vice-President JD Vance's work on Iran, saying that he “has done a very good job on Iran.”

items.title: Dow Jones Industrial Average edges lower as Goldman drags, Crude Oil climbs back over $100
items.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) edged lower on Monday, slipping around 50 points to trade near 47,900 after recovering from a much steeper drop at the open. The S&P 500 rose 0.2% to trade above 6,800, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.6% to push above 23,000.

items.title: Germany: Recovery delayed by energy shock – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank economists say Germany’s recovery is being pushed back by higher energy costs and uncertainty linked to the Middle East conflict. They cut their 2026 growth forecast to 1.0% while keeping 2027 at 1.5%. Inflation is projected to average 2.7% this year.

items.title: WTI Oil surges on renewed Iran tensions as US blockade threat stokes supply fears
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) US Oil trades around $95.70 per barrel on Monday at the time of writing, rising 5.90% on the day but still struggling to regain the $100 threshold after last week’s sharp volatility.

items.title: USD: Blockade supports cautious rebound – Scotiabank
items.summary: Scotiabank strategists Shaun Osborne and Eric Theoret note the US Dollar (USD) is firmer after President Trump announced a blockade of Iranian ports, with US Dollar Index (DXY) off its Asian highs and still seen within a broader bearish setup.

items.title: Oil: Prices surge on Hormuz blockade threat – ING
items.summary: ING’s Ewa Manthey and Warren Patterson report that Oil markets rallied sharply after US-Iran talks collapsed, with ICE Brent jumping over 9% and NYMEX WTI moving above $105/bbl. The US plans a maritime blockade around Iranian ports, stoking supply fears.

items.title: Fed: On hold as energy shock lifts inflation – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank economists report the Federal Reserve (Fed) left rates at 3.50%-3.75% in March and still expects a 25 bp cut in September. Markets price only modest easing. They warn a prolonged Strait of Hormuz blockade could push Brent toward USD 120.

items.title: Canada: Carney majority prospects and fiscal outlook – TD Securities
items.summary: TD Securities strategists discuss Canadian by-elections in three federal ridings and notes that Prime Minister Carney’s Liberals are close to securing a majority in Parliament.

items.title: US Dollar Index rebounds to 99.00 as oil price recovery prompts hawkish Fed risks
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which gauges the Greenback’s value against six major currencies, trades 0.25% higher to near 99.00 during the European trading session on Monday.

items.title: WTI Crude Oil stalls below $98.00 with Iran peace hopes still alive
items.summary: Oil prices jumped on Monday following the failure of the peace talks between the US and Iran and the US pledge to block the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: HUF: Election outcome boosts Forint as EU risk recedes – Danske Bank
items.summary: Danske Research Team reports that Hungary’s election delivered a decisive victory for Péter Magyar’s pro‑EU Tisza party over Viktor Orban’s Fidesz, easing concerns about future EU budget and Ukraine aid negotiations.

items.title: S&P 500: Earnings optimism versus conflict shock – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank’s strategists highlight that S&P 500 futures are lower as the Iran conflict escalates and energy prices jump, pressuring global risk sentiment.

items.title: Australian Dollar trades mixed on renewed Middle East conflicts, job data awaited
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) demonstrates a mixed performance against its major currency peers, still trading 0.2% lower to near 0.7050 against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Monday, even after recovering a major chunk of its opening losses.

items.title: Canadian Dollar pares losses vs USD as rallying oil prices counter hawkish Fed bets
items.summary: The USD/CAD pair retreats a few pips from the Asian session high and currently trades around the 1.3860-1.3855 region, up around 0.15% for the day.

items.title: WTI Price Forecast: Rallies to $98 as Trump aims to blockade Hormuz
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), futures on NYMEX, trade 7.6% higher to near $98.00 during the Asian trading session on Monday.

items.title: PBOC sets USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8657 vs. 6.8654 previous
items.summary: On Monday, the People’s Bank of China (PBOC) sets the USD/CNY central rate for the trading session ahead at 6.8657 compared to Friday's of 6.8654 and 6.8395 Reuters estimate.

items.title: NZD/USD recovers slightly from daily low, keeps the red above 0.5800 on firmer USD
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair opens with a bearish gap at the start of a new trading week in reaction to failed US-Iran peace talks over the weekend and trades around the 0.5800 mark during the Asian session.

items.title: Canadian Dollar falls further as USD haven demand counters WTI surge
items.summary: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) is extending its pullback from two-week highs of 1.3844 against the US Dollar (USD) early Monday, with USD/CAD capitalizing on the renewed demand for the Greenback as a go-to safe-haven.

items.title: WTI jumps roughly 8% toward $100 as US blockades Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) – the US oil benchmark – has opened the week with a bullish gap, climbing roughly 8%, looking to retarget the $100 threshold.

items.title: Japanese Yen falls as US Dollar rises after US–Iran talks collapse
items.summary: USD/JPY extends its winning streak for the third successive day, trading around 159.80 during the Asian hours on Monday.

items.title: US President Trump considers limited strikes on Iran after talks collapse - WSJ
items.summary: Citing officials and people familiar with the situation, the Wall Street Journal (WSJ) reported on Monday that White House advisers are considering resuming limited military strikes in Iran along with the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz as a way to break a deadlock in peace talks.
